AI & Machine Learning March 13, 2026 · 8 min read

NVIDIA Announces Blackwell Ultra: 2x Performance for AI Training at Half the Power

NVIDIA's latest GPU architecture promises to reshape the AI training landscape with unprecedented efficiency gains. The Blackwell Ultra series, announced at GTC 2026, delivers twice the training performance while consuming 50% less power than its predecessor.

Share Tweet LinkedIn
Read full article →
AI March 13, 2026 · 5 min read

Anthropic Releases Claude 5: First AI to Pass the Turing Test in Peer-Reviewed Study

In a landmark moment for artificial intelligence, Anthropic's Claude 5 has become the first AI system to consistently pass the Turing test in a rigorous, peer-reviewed double-blind study conducted by MIT researchers.

Read more →
Mobile March 12, 2026 · 6 min read

iPhone 17 Pro Leak: Under-Display Face ID and 48MP Periscope Lens Confirmed

Reliable supply chain sources have confirmed that Apple's upcoming iPhone 17 Pro will feature under-display Face ID technology, eliminating the Dynamic Island entirely for a truly full-screen design.

Read more →
Security March 12, 2026 · 4 min read

Critical Chrome Zero-Day Actively Exploited: Update Immediately

Google has released an emergency patch for a critical zero-day vulnerability in Chrome that is being actively exploited in the wild. The flaw affects the V8 JavaScript engine and allows remote code execution.

Read more →
Cloud March 11, 2026 · 7 min read

AWS Graviton 5 Benchmarks Show 40% Lead Over Intel Xeon in Cloud Workloads

Amazon's latest custom ARM processor, the Graviton 5, is showing remarkable performance in early benchmarks, outpacing Intel's latest Xeon chips by up to 40% in key cloud computing workloads.

Read more →
Startups March 11, 2026 · 3 min read

OpenAI Closes $15B Round at $350B Valuation, Plans IPO for 2027

OpenAI has closed its latest funding round, raising $15 billion at a staggering $350 billion valuation. The company also confirmed plans for an initial public offering in late 2027.
